item.title: Fans Think Resident Evil’s Leon Would Be A Cornball In Every Universe
item.content:encoded:
Resident Evil fans love Leon Kennedy for his good looks, constant aura farming, and his inability to stop cracking wise-ass one-liners during even the most dire of circumstances. The ex-cop loves a well-timed pun or quip as he’s wrecking shop while fighting through the horrors. He brings a bit of levity to games that can be terrifying, and we appreciate that. But what if he brought that same jarring whimsy to other series? That’s the thought experiment Resident Evil fans are working through right now.

In Resident Evil Requiem, Leon is pretty much an unstoppable force, capable of parrying literal rockets with a swing of his hatchet. So what’s stopping him from being plopped into any other universe and leaving a path of destruction in his wake, all while making innocent passersby groan at his puns? Only Capcom not signing whatever contracts it would take to put Leon in these series. Here are a few of my favorites.

“Not very Super if you ask me, just Saiyan” https://t.co/ik6RQcrHrY

— RESIDENT EVIL (@REBiohazardNews) March 12, 2026

“Sorry Dino, but we don’t need another Crisis.”

*backflips* https://t.co/thyoD9eybx pic.twitter.com/alNUJcE1dh

— The Photoshop Guy (@Photoshop_Gy) March 12, 2026

if these two fought you JUST KNOW Leon would kick his ass and then say some dumb shit like "Playtime's over" pic.twitter.com/CGv2T8FsPR

— phisnom 🧪⚠️ (@phisnom) March 11, 2026

“I’m flattered, but I don’t think we’re gonna click.” pic.twitter.com/IzFsFZP5Gd

— RESIDENT EVIL (@REBiohazardNews) March 11, 2026

“This hill is awfully Silent”

*does a backflip* https://t.co/paWviQzSVj

— Tangle #1 Fan 🤍🩶🤍 (CROSSWORLDS ERA) (@OhnimusP3767) March 11, 2026

*Leon sees a clicker*
Leon: "You don't seem like a fungi."
*Proceeds to suplex*
Leon: "But maybe you're starting to grow on me." https://t.co/SutxV50Nfb

— Nerbit (@Nerbit_13098) March 11, 2026

Shoot the knees + roundhouse
“I heard you’re hungry. Chew on this.” https://t.co/2IOL11AD72 pic.twitter.com/fEYFVOWgQP

— Liam Swan (@LiamRichardSwan) March 12, 2026

"So it's a mimic. What, no original ideas?"

*GKFKCIFMFKDKVK*

"Sorry pal, but no one copies me." pic.twitter.com/h52NVInlBM

— DMuted (@MutedNotSuited) March 12, 2026

See Resident Evil fans, this is certainly a more fun use of the internet than tearing one another apart over who Leon may or may not be married to. Follow Leon’s lead and choose joy over hate, or puns over entertaining freaks who think that Leon is married to Sherry. You have the free will to make good posts.



https://kotaku.com/resident-evil-requiem-leon-one-liners-last-us-silent-hill-2000678374

item.title: Battle Royale Games Collapse as Sandbox Titles Soar Amid New Trends
item.content:encoded:
It’s finally being confirmed in the data. The world is moving on from battle royale titles and is instead focusing on sandbox games, adopting that sense of freedom and choice over being the last man standing. In a new report from NewZoo, it was confirmed that battle royale games saw a monumental drop of -27% in playtime in 2025 versus 2024.

By contrast, sandbox titles grew a whopping 36% in playtime in the same window. There were also some minor declines in the shooter and role-playing space, while adventure games also saw minor growth.

Survival in a Different Way

It seems to be the new trend these days. We’re seeing survival-sandbox games soar in almost every respect, as players settle in and build a life for themselves in some digital universe, often opposed by a traditional threat: zombies, raiders, the elements, and so on. That’s how most sandbox games work.

Battle royale titles, which saw a boom from 2019 onwards with the likes of Fortnite, Apex Legends, and Call of Duty: Warzone skyrocketing, had a monumental drop across all platforms in 2025. The decline isn’t much of a surprise, as it’s effectively common knowledge that the battle royale experience is dying out.

Even Fortnite is leaning into alternate entertainment to keep fans engaged, standing up the UEFN ecosystem and offering all kinds of games and side modes away from the battle royale core.

It gets even more impressive when you realise that sandbox games are flying up over every other genre despite some major releases in the last year. For instance, Battlefield 6, the best-selling game of last year, a first-person shooter, and ARC Raiders, a transformative extraction shooter that racked up millions of sales, both dropped in 2025.

It’s not a good look for these top-tier battle royale games, which are failing to secure traction going forward. Apex Legends had a 24% drop in playtime in 2025, and Fortnite recorded a 29% drop. Shooters also suffered immensely:

  • Call of Duty: -33%
  • VALORANT: -15%
  • Rainbow Six Siege: -24%
  • Overwatch: -29%
  • Destiny 2: -40%

Even Counter-Strike and Marvel Rivals couldn’t save shooters, and that’s with Marvel Rivals seeing a 546% increase in players in 2025. Although that figure is skewed, given that Marvel Rivals dropped in December 2024.

The numbers for 2026 will be much more interesting to view when they’re released, given that ARC Raiders has blown up and will understandably bolster results.

Are you surprised to learn that battle royale games are disappearing? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the recently proposed Tarkov changes

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Can You Preload Crimson Desert?
item.content:encoded:
The excitement is really mounting surrounding Crimson Desert, as the upcoming open-world behemoth is almost ready to tackle. It’s the kind of release that fans will call in sick for school or work for, but part of maximising the play time is making sure it’s ready for launch.

Some developers allow you to preload the game before launch, so as soon as the clock strikes release time, the game is ready for you to play from minute one. Many out there will be hoping that Pearl Abyss will offer the same treatment for Crimson Desert, and if you’re one of them, there’s some good news for you down below.

Can You Preload Crimson Desert Before Launch?

Yes, you can preload Crimson Desert before launch, meaning you’ll be able to have the game downloaded and ready for its March 19 launch day.

Crimson Desert is having a simultaneous worldwide release, meaning it’ll go live across all devices everywhere in the world at the same time, regardless of time zones. Therefore, you could be playing it at the crack of dawn or the middle of the day, but it’s certain that it’ll be at the same time as everybody else.

  • More Insider Gaming: Crimson Desert Release Date Countdown

That means that some fans won’t be able to spoil the opening hours for those who might have to wait. Instead, everybody can dive into the world of Pywel together, and here are the exact times you can prepare for that journey.

When Can You Preload Crimson Desert?

You can preload Crimson Desert two days before its official launch, meaning it’ll be available for download on March 17.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Crimson Desert Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices

As mentioned before, the game will have a worldwide release, so that date might be different depending on where you live. Thankfully, Insider Gaming has broken down all of the preload times across a whole variety of time zones, so you can plan your adventures accordingly:

Are you excited to finally play Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the Crimson Desert launch trailer, as well as whether it’s a sequel or prequel to Black Desert Online.

item.title: Tropico 7 PC closed beta test begins March 31
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Sign-ups now available.

Publisher Kalypso Media and developer Gaming Minds Studios will host a closed beta test for Tropico 7 for PC via Steam and Microsoft Store beginning March 31, the companies announced. It will run for two weeks. Sign-ups are available now until March 22.

The announcement reads, “The playtest focuses on gathering feedback on core gameplay systems, political mechanics, and two selected campaign parts from the Colonial Times and World Wars and two scenario missions in the Cold War and Modern Times. The Closed Beta supports English and German text.”

Tropico 7 is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, Microsoft Store in 2026.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/tropico-7-pc-closed-beta-test-begins-march-31

item.title: Trash Goblin coming to PS5, Xbox Series, and Switch on March 26
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Currently available for PC.

Spilt Milk Studios will release cozy shopkeeping game Trash Goblin for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and Switch on March 26, the companies announced.

Trash Goblin first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on November 6, 2024, followed by a full release on May 28, 2025. The PC version will also be made available via Microsoft Store alongside the consoles launch.

“We’re incredibly excited to finally bring Trash Goblin to console players,” said Spilt Milk Studios creative director Andrew Smith in a press release. “The support from our community since launch has been incredible, and reaching consoles marks a major milestone for the game. We can’t wait to welcome new goblin shopkeepers and let them discover the joy of cleaning, restoring, and upcycling their own curious trinkets!”

Here is an overview of the game, via Spilt Milk Studios:

About

The console version launches with the 1.4 “New Horizons” update, first released on PC via Steam, adding new quests, returning characters, trinkets, polish improvements, and more.

Trash Goblin arrives on Xbox Series X|S, PlayStation 5 and Nintendo Switch with the full cozy shopkeeping experience. Play as a goblin shopkeeper who uncovers buried trinkets, cleaning and restoring them before combining them into strange new creations to satisfy peculiar customers. With hundreds of trinkets to discover, dozens of colorful NPCs to meet and a shop to customize and expand, Trash Goblin offers a relaxed, creative restoration loop. The console release also includes the recent “New Horizons” 1.4 update, adding new quests with beloved returning characters, trinkets, new cosmetics, Achievements and further improvements.

Trash Goblin lets you customize your myriad of already-peculiar goods by combining these trinkets together in almost endless ways, upcycling them into even more strange and wonderful new items to meet the eccentric requests of your colorful customers. Get to know your regulars so you can make custom pieces tailored around their desires—which will fetch you even more money!

Grow your business! Spend your savings to upgrade your shop, buy new and better tools that offer additional ways to restore your trinkets—making them even more valuable—and expand your space, customizing it to your tastes.

Key Features

  • 339 Trinkets and Accessories with 1000s of combinations and variations to find, clean, upcycle, sell, and collect.
  • Multiple Tools to buy, upgrade and use across three Trinket-care minigames.
  • Make life easier with the Coffee Pot, Soak Tub, and other handy gadgets.
  • Expand your shop with shelves, storage, and more.
  • Four distinct locations to visit and sell in.
  • Customise your shop with cosmetics covering bedclothes, wallpapers, and more.
  • 40 unique NPC characters to get to know and sell to.
  • Over 120 Quests to complete.
  • 40 Achievements to earn.
  • Over 20 hours of story content.
  • Endless randomised customers.
  • Endless gameplay.

Watch a new trailer below.

Consoles Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/trash-goblin-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-and-switch-on-march-26
